name: hcportal_getEvent
description: Retrieves event details by publicEventId, kennelUniqueShortName + runDesignator, or special keywords 'nextrun'/'lastrun'. Returns up to 3 rowsets: event details, attendee list (without payment info), and optionally attendee list with full payment details (if @includePaymentInfo=1). Used by both admin portal and public HashRuns.org site. Auth token validation is commented out (not enforced).

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: user's public hasher ID. Two service account GUIDs are accepted: 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 (public/HashRuns.org) and 22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222 (admin portal). Determines isAdmin flag and attendee rowset visibility.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token. Token validation is commented out (lines 148-164) so this parameter is effectively unused.

parameters.name: includePaymentInfo
parameters.description: Flag: 0=return attendee list without payment columns, 1=return attendee list with full payment details (amounts, confirmation dates, payment types). Only returned when caller is not a service account.

parameters.name: publicEventId
parameters.description: Optional: direct lookup by event's public ID. If provided, this takes precedence over kennelUniqueShortName/runDesignator. Validation of this parameter is commented out (lines 69-83).

parameters.name: kennelUniqueShortName
parameters.description: Optional: kennel identifier. Used with @runDesignator to resolve event. Required when using runDesignator lookup.

parameters.name: runDesignator
parameters.description: Optional: event identifier within a kennel. Special values: 'nextrun' (next future event >4hrs from now), 'lastrun' (most recent past event). Otherwise treated as EventNumber cast to string for exact match lookup.

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Used in LOG.GeneralLog message. COALESCE to '<no IP address>' if NULL.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Stored in LOG.GeneralLog.Data field. Falls back to placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Uses errorType 2 (validation). Errors: null/invalid publicHasherId, publicHasherId not found in Hasher table, event not found by any lookup method. Validation does not short-circuit: multiple error rowsets are possible if multiple checks fail. Each error is logged to HC.ErrorLog.

rowsets.name: EventDetails
rowsets.description: Returned when event is found. Contains comprehensive event data including kennel info, location, pricing, dissemination settings, integration details, and currency formatting info. Single row.

columns.name: kennelUniqueShortName
columns.description: Kennel.KennelUniqueShortName (derived from table structure, not explicitly in query)

columns.name: extEventStartDatetime
columns.description: Event.FbEventStartDatetime converted to datetime2 (timezone conversion hack - should eventually send UTC + offset separately)

columns.name: eventStartDatetime
columns.description: Event.EventStartDatetime converted to datetime2 (timezone conversion hack - should eventually send UTC + offset separately)

columns.name: eventEndDatetime
columns.description: Event.EventEndDatetime converted to datetime2 (timezone conversion hack - should eventually send UTC + offset separately)

columns.name: countryId
columns.description: Event.CountryId (country where kennel was located when event was created, may differ from current kennel location)

columns.name: searchText
columns.description: Empty string hardcoded (placeholder for future search functionality)

rowsets.name: AttendeeListBasic
rowsets.description: Returned when @publicHasherId is NOT one of the two service account GUIDs and @includePaymentInfo = 0. Contains basic attendee info without payment details. Multiple rows (one per attendee).

columns.name: displayName
columns.description: COALESCE(HasherEventMap.DisplayName, Hasher.DisplayName) - override display name from event map or computed from Hasher

columns.name: amountPaidStr
columns.description: COALESCE(formatted currency string with Payment.CreditAmount, empty string). Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount in currency symbol template.

columns.name: amountOwedStr
columns.description: COALESCE(formatted currency string with Payment.DebitAmount, empty string). Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount in currency symbol template.

columns.name: creditAvailableStr
columns.description: COALESCE(formatted currency string with Payment.CreditAvailable, empty string). Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount in currency symbol template.

rowsets.name: AttendeeListWithPayment
rowsets.description: Returned when @publicHasherId is NOT one of the two service account GUIDs and @includePaymentInfo = 1. Contains full attendee info including payment details. Multiple rows (one per attendee).

columns.name: cancelledBy
columns.description: Hasher.DisplayName (computed column) of user who cancelled payment

columns.name: amountPaidStr
columns.description: Formatted currency string with Payment.CreditAmount. Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount.

columns.name: amountOwedStr
columns.description: Formatted currency string with Payment.DebitAmount. Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount.

columns.name: netPaymentStr
columns.description: Formatted currency string with Payment.NetPayment. Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount.

columns.name: creditAvailableStr
columns.description: Formatted currency string with Payment.CreditAvailable. Uses REPLACE to substitute '^' with formatted amount.

columns.name: paymentConfirmedBy
columns.description: Hasher.DisplayName (computed column) of user who confirmed payment

columns.name: paymentType
columns.description: Payment.PaymentType (0=Unknown, 1=Not paid, 2=Free, 3=Cash, 4=Bank transfer, 5=Cash other amount, 6=Hash credit, 7=Bank transfer other amount)

columns.name: paymentTypeString
columns.description: Human-readable payment type string decoded from Payment.PaymentType via CASE statement

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation failures (null/invalid publicHasherId, publicHasherId not found, event not found)
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call (with IP data, error title if error occurred, event ID in StrParam1)

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If queries fail (e.g. FK constraint issues), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.
ErrorLog and GeneralLog inserts are not in a transaction. If the SP fails after logging, partial inserts persist.

Lines 148-164: HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN validation is commented out. The @accessToken parameter is accepted but never validated, meaning anyone with a valid publicHasherId can access any event data without token verification.

All validation blocks run regardless of prior failures. Each failed check SELECTs an error rowset but does not RETURN. If multiple validations fail, the caller receives multiple error rowsets, and @isError flag is overwritten. The SP continues to execute logic after setting @isError=1.
Line 30: Uses DATALENGTH(@publicHasherId) != 16 to validate GUID. DATALENGTH is correct for UNIQUEIDENTIFIER (16 bytes) but inconsistent with broader project guidance to prefer LEN for string checks.

codeSmells.issue: Hardcoded magic GUIDs for service accounts
codeSmells.detail: Lines 47, 167, 315: Only 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 and 22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222 are accepted as service account GUIDs. No documentation of what each represents or why these specific values.

Lines 69-83: Validation for @publicEventId is commented out. The parameter is accepted and used, but NULL values are not flagged as errors. This creates inconsistent behavior - the SP will attempt to resolve the event but may silently fail.

Lines 256-258: CONVERT(datetime2, ...) with inline comment acknowledging this is a hack. SP returns local time instead of UTC + offset, requiring clients to reverse-engineer timezone. TODO comment suggests future fix.

codeSmells.issue: Complex event lookup logic without explicit errors
codeSmells.detail: Lines 88-130: Event resolution has 4 paths (publicEventId, nextrun, lastrun, eventNumber). If multiple parameters are provided, precedence is implicit (publicEventId > runDesignator check). No error if conflicting parameters are provided.
Lines 106, 123: DATEADD(hour, 4, ...) adds 4 hours to event start time for 'nextrun'/'lastrun' filtering. No comment explaining why 4 hours or what timezone assumption this makes.

codeSmells.issue: AT TIME ZONE conversions may fail on invalid timezone
codeSmells.detail: Lines 106, 123: Uses AT TIME ZONE with tz.Timezone from DomainValues.Timezone table. If timezone string is invalid or unrecognized by SQL Server, query will fail without TRY/CATCH to handle it.
Line 305: searchText column hardcoded to empty string. Column appears to be a placeholder for future functionality but adds unnecessary overhead to every result.
Lines 92, 109: LOWER(@rundesignator) = 'nextrun' or 'lastrun'. Case-insensitive comparison forces function on parameter, though this is less critical since @runDesignator is small.

codeSmells.issue: Type mismatch: EventNumber cast to string
codeSmells.detail: Line 129: CAST(evt.EventNumber as nvarchar(50)) = @runDesignator. EventNumber is SMALLINT in table, cast to string for comparison with NVARCHAR(50) parameter. More efficient to cast parameter to SMALLINT if not 'nextrun'/'lastrun'.

codeSmells.issue: Inconsistent NULL handling in currency formatting
codeSmells.detail: Lines 325-327: Basic attendee list uses COALESCE(formatted string, '') for payment amounts. If Payment record is NULL, COALESCE resolves to empty string. AttendeeListWithPayment rowset (lines 353-356) omits outer COALESCE, may return NULL strings if Payment is NULL.

codeSmells.issue: LEFT OUTER JOIN with IS NULL filter may be inefficient
codeSmells.detail: Lines 335, 379: LEFT OUTER JOIN HC.Payment WHERE ... AND pay.CancelledBy_UserId IS NULL. Filter on LEFT JOIN may force scan. Consider filtering cancelled payments in subquery or using NOT EXISTS.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
Never change the precedence order of event lookup methods (publicEventId > runDesignator logic)
Never change the magic GUID values for service accounts without coordinating with all callers
Never change the @includePaymentInfo=0 vs =1 rowset structure (column set must remain compatible)
